documentation.value: A mapping (dictionary/hash) associates keys with values using a colon followed by a space.
A scalar key (string, number, or boolean). Complex keys use `? key`
Any YAML value: scalar, sequence, or nested mapping
A sequence (list/array) is indicated by a dash followed by a space for each entry.
Any YAML value: scalar, mapping, or nested sequence
Scalar values are plain text, quoted strings, numbers, booleans (`true`/`false`), or null. YAML auto-detects types.
Plain: `hello`, single-quoted: `'hello'`, double-quoted: `"hello\n"`
Integer: `42`, float: `3.14`, hex: `0xFF`, octal: `0o77`
`true`, `false`, `yes`, `no`, `on`, `off` (unquoted)
`null`, `~`, or empty value
An anchor (`&name`) marks a node for later reuse. An alias (`*name`) references the anchored node.
Defines an anchor on a node, e.g. `&defaults` on a mapping
References a previously defined anchor, e.g. `*defaults`
Tags specify the data type of a node. Custom tags use `!` prefix, standard tags use `!!`.
A type tag: `!!str`, `!!int`, `!!float`, `!!bool`, `!!null`, `!!seq`, `!!map`, or custom `!MyType`
The value to interpret according to the tag
Merges the referenced mapping into the current mapping. Keys in the current mapping take precedence.
An alias referencing an anchored mapping to merge, or a sequence of aliases
Block scalars preserve (`|`, literal) or fold (`>`, folded) newlines in multiline text.
Literal block: preserves newlines as-is. Use `|-` to strip trailing newline, `|+` to keep all trailing newlines.
Folded block: folds newlines into spaces. Use `>-` to strip trailing newline, `>+` to keep all trailing newlines.
`---` starts a new YAML document. `...` ends a document. Multiple documents can exist in one file.
Marks the beginning of a YAML document
Marks the end of a YAML document (optional)
